title: Tapping to Integrate Calm Forever
subtitle: A bedtime tapping practice for anchoring the peace built over 30 days as a permanent way of being
excerpt: A five-minute bedtime tapping practice from Day 30 of a 30-day sleep programme. The script closes the programme by marking what the body has become: slower breath, lighter shoulders, nights that feel like home. The reframe anchors the change as permanent — calm carried forward, not a state to be re-achieved.
sourceNotes: The tapping framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique (EFT), in use since the mid-1990s. The script is adapted from the Day 30 night tap of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive to Fall Asleep Faster and Wake Energised (Rebecca J Page, 2025, ISBN 9798269649795).

practice.whenToUse: Use on the final night of a 30-day programme, or any time a period of sustained inner work deserves to be closed and integrated rather than simply ended.

content.text: A five-minute bedtime tapping practice from Day 30 of a 30-day sleep programme. The final night tap closes the programme by naming what the body has already become: breath that is slower, shoulders that carry less, nights that feel like home rather than something to get through. The reframe anchors calm as permanent — a change that carries forward into every night that follows.
New to tapping? Read
how EFT tapping works
first.
The practice
Karate chop
Even though I used to believe sleep was something I had to chase, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though I feared I'd never fix it, I honour that I've proven otherwise.
Even though this is the final night of this programme, I'm open to letting this peace stay for good.
Tapping round
Eyebrow: I can feel how different I am now.
Side of eye: My breath is slower, my shoulders lighter.
Under eye: My body trusts me again.
Under nose: My nights feel like home.
Chin: I am the change I worked toward.
Collarbone: Rest comes to me; I don't need to chase it.
Under arm: This calm is mine to keep.
Top of head: I'm safe, whole, and deeply at peace.
Reframe to positive
Eyebrow: I live in alignment with rest.
Side of eye: Sleep comes naturally and stays.
Under eye: My mind, body, and energy flow together.
Under nose: I wake renewed and grateful each morning.
Chin: This peace is permanent.
Collarbone: I've anchored safety into my body.
Under arm: I am calm, capable, and rested.
Top of head: I sleep with ease tonight, and every night that follows.
Tap each round three times, then move to the reframe and tap that three times.
Where this practice comes from

The tapping framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique (EFT), developed in the mid-1990s from Roger Callahan's Thought Field Therapy. The script is adapted from the Day 30 night tap of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
